# ADR-003: Locale-Aware Parsing as the Canonical Entry Point

<!-- Generated by seed-golden-context | Last updated: 2026-05-11 -->

## Status

Accepted

## Context

In Contentful, entries and assets have localized fields. When a Rich Text node contains an embedded entry or asset, the parser must resolve that link against the correct locale to return the right field values. The original `parse()` method had no locale parameter, so embedded references were always resolved in the space's default locale — regardless of the locale the application was actually rendering for.

This caused a bug ([#65](https://github.com/contentful/rich-text.php/issues/65)) where pages rendered in non-default locales would show embedded content in the wrong locale.

## Decision

In v4.0.0, the `ParserInterface` was extended with locale-aware variants:
- `parseLocalized(array $data, ?string $locale): NodeInterface` — canonical entry point
- `parseCollectionLocalized(array $data, ?string $locale): array` — canonical collection entry point

The original `parse()` and `parseCollection()` methods are retained for backward compatibility but **deprecated**. They internally call `parseLocalized($data, null)`, which resolves links in the default locale.

The `NodeMapperInterface` was updated to include the `$locale` parameter in `map()`, so all mapper implementations receive and propagate the locale during recursive parsing.

## Consequences

- Applications that pass the correct locale to `parseLocalized()` get correctly localized embedded content.
- Applications using the deprecated `parse()` get the old (default-locale) behavior — no regression, but they miss out on the fix.
- This was a **breaking change** to `ParserInterface` and `NodeMapperInterface` — any application with a custom parser or mapper must update signatures. Documented in the v4.0.0 CHANGELOG.
- PHP 7 support was dropped in the same v4.0.0 release to allow use of modern PHP 8 type declarations.
- The `?string $locale` parameter on `parseLocalized()` allows `null` to be passed explicitly, which resolves to the default locale — preserving backward-compatible behavior for callers that don't have a locale context.
